humanSummary.text: Initial audit found a P1 marker-only Wolf detection overclaim. The branch repaired that issue and re-audit found no remaining P0/P1 blockers. Non-detected variant clarity and split Wolf primary-evidence tests are recorded as follow-ups.

findings.title: Non-detected archive variant normalization
findings.description: Non-detected archive rows can still serialize a detectedVariant value such as `wolf-protected-archive` despite `detected: false`. No match, signals, requirements, diagnostics, or capabilities leak, but normalizing variants for non-detected rows would reduce consumer confusion.

evidence.detail: Kaifuu re-audit accepted the P1 repair while noting non-detected rows still carry detectedVariant values.

findings.impact: Consumers may misread a non-detected row's variant field as a weak positive signal.
actionableAcceptanceCriteria: Non-detected archive rows omit detectedVariant or use an explicit unknown/non-detected variant.
Regression tests cover marker-only Wolf rows and other non-detected archive rows.

proposedDagNode.title: Non-detected archive variant normalization
proposedDagNode.summary: Normalize archive detection variant fields on non-detected rows so marker-only rows cannot imply weak positive support.
deliverables: Archive detection row serialization change for non-detected variants
Regression tests for marker-only Wolf and other non-detected archive rows
acceptanceCriteria: Non-detected archive rows cannot expose a detected variant that looks like positive support.
Known marker-only subtype rows still report unknown-variant diagnostics where appropriate.
auditFocus: Non-detected archive row variant field implying support

findings.title: Split Wolf primary evidence regression tests
findings.description: Current tests prove valid combined Wolf primary evidence still works and code inspection confirms OR logic. Separate extension-only and header-only tests would protect the stricter predicate.

evidence.detail: Kaifuu re-audit recommended separate tests for `.wolf` extension-only and `WOLF RPG Editor` header-only primary evidence.

findings.impact: A future predicate refactor could accidentally require both extension and header evidence or weaken the predicate without a focused failure.
actionableAcceptanceCriteria: Wolf detection tests cover `.wolf` extension-only primary evidence.
Wolf detection tests cover `WOLF RPG Editor` header-only primary evidence.

proposedDagNode.title: Wolf primary evidence split tests
proposedDagNode.summary: Add focused Wolf archive detection tests for extension-only and header-only primary evidence.
deliverables: Wolf detection regression for `.wolf` extension-only evidence
Wolf detection regression for `WOLF RPG Editor` header-only evidence
acceptanceCriteria: Valid Wolf detection still works with extension-only primary evidence.
Valid Wolf detection still works with header-only primary evidence.
